channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Pope Francis' doctor says pontiff isn't out of danger but that his condition isn't life-threatening
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis’ condition isn't life-threatening, but he's not out of danger, his medical team said Friday as the 88-year-old pontiff marked his first week in a hospital with pneumonia on top of chronic bronchitis.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Texas police charged child after school shooting false alarm
item.description: Last September, school districts across the state dealt with a cascade of threats. At least 17 Central Texas school districts fielded 56 threats in September alone, according to records obtained by KXAN through the Texas Public Information Act.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Wiley, NewsNation

item.title: Rwanda expresses solidarity with military general sanctioned by the US over violence in Congo
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Rwanda's government on Friday asserted solidarity with a top official sanctioned by the U.S. over violence in eastern Congo, where Rwanda-backed rebels have captured two major cities in an armed conflict that now threatens the Congolese government.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI and RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) threw cold water on the idea of using savings from the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) government-slashing activities to send checks to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, ABC 4

item.title: Grand jury recommends Alabama police department be abolished
item.description: The small Alabama city of Hanceville, located about 45 miles north of Birmingham, placed its entire police force on administrative leave a day after the grand jury recommendation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Federal judge allows Trump’s mass firings of federal workers to continue
item.description: U.S. District Judge Christopher Cooper decided he could not grant a motion from unions representing the workers to temporarily block President Donald Trump’s mass firings of federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Their mosque burned down in LA-area wildfire. They're still determined to gather for Ramadan
item.description: Members of Masjid Al-Taqwa, a mosque which burned down in one of the recent Los Angeles-area wildfires, are scrambling to find a space where they can gather, pray and support one another during the holy month of Ramadan.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Bharath, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Luigi Mangione set for first court appearance since his arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: The man accused of fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare in New York City and leading authorities on a five-day manhunt is scheduled to be in court Friday for the first time since his December arraignment on state murder and terror charges.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Singer of Canadian anthem at 4 Nations Face-Off changes lyric to protest Trump's 51st state remarks
item.description: The anthem singer who performed the Canadian anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off championship game Thursday night changed a lyric in “O Canada” as a response to U.S. President Donald Trump's repeated remarks about making the country the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO and KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former Kentucky AG Daniel Cameron launches Senate bid
item.description: Prior to becoming Kentucky’s first Black attorney general, Cameron served as legal counsel to McConnell, the state’s longest serving senator, for two years.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Spurs star Victor Wembanyama to miss remainder of season after blood clot diagnosis
item.description: San Antonio star Victor Wembanyama's season is over after he was diagnosed with a blood clot in his right shoulder, a condition that will sideline one of the league's brightest stars and is the second massive health-related hit for the Spurs in a matter of months.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Judge rules Trump administration can move ahead on mass government layoffs
item.description: A federal judge Thursday refused a group of government employee unions’ request to block the Trump administration from moving ahead with plans to dramatically reduce the federal workforce. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Some New York prison guards charged in beating death of handcuffed inmate appear in court
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Six New York prison guards have been indicted for second-degree murder in the beating death of a handcuffed inmate, a brutal incident captured on body-worn cameras that triggered widespread outrage and calls for justice.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: IRS layoffs could hurt revenue collection and foil efforts to go after rich tax dodgers, experts say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The layoffs of roughly 7,000 IRS probationary workers beginning this week likely mean the end of the agency's plan to go after high-wealth tax dodgers and could spell disaster for revenue collections, experts say.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kids' disability rights cases stalled as Trump began to overhaul Education Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It was obvious to Christine Smith Olsey that her son was not doing well at school, despite educators telling her to leave it to the experts. The second-grade student stumbled over words, and other kids teased him so much he started to call himself “an idiot.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, COLLIN BINKLEY and ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Owners of Abby's Bakery in Los Fresnos charged with harboring illegal migrants
item.description: According to the complaint, the apprehension of the individuals was the result of a worksite enforcement action.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Yañez, NewsNation

item.title: Smithfield fined $10K after worker locked in oven
item.description: "An employee performing work inside Smokehouse #18 became entrapped after the doors were closed and sealed," reads the citation.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Newton, NewsNation

item.title: With Welcome Center closed in Brownsville, NGO focusing on helping migrants south of border
item.description: A migrant shelter in this border town has closed after a sudden decrease in asylum seekers due to new immigration policies implemented by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli UFC fighter talks antisemitism at self-defense seminar as Hamas hostages mark 500 days
item.description: Israeli UFC fighter Natan Levy marks 500th day of Hamas holding hostages with seminar aimed at teaching Jews how to defend themselves from possible antisemitic attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida leaders warn deadly consequences of ‘senior assassin’ game
item.description: They are urging parents and guardians to discuss with their children the potential dangers of playing this game.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Muller, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s vow to preserve Medicaid collides with House GOP plan for tax cuts
item.description: President Trump’s vow to keep Medicaid intact is colliding headlong into the Republicans’ strategy to extend their 2017 tax cuts, complicating the GOP effort to secure a major policy win in the early months of Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Senate GOP dismayed by Trump, Zelensky war of words
item.description: Public comments from President Trump blaming Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky for Russia’s invasion of his country are unnerving Senate Republicans, who have largely sought to avoid conflicts with the White House.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell won't seek reelection to Senate
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) announced Thursday that he is not seeking reelection in 2026, bringing an end to the longest-serving Senate leader’s political career.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KRON4

item.title: Man who admitted to shooting Black teen at wrong door dies before sentencing
item.description: Andrew Lester, a man who pleaded guilty last week to second-degree assault after he shot Ralph Yarl, an unarmed teenage boy in Kansas City in 2023, has died, according to a Clay County prosecutor.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, ABC 4

item.title: This rookie QB card could be worth $50K if you pull it from a new Topps set
item.description: Denver Broncos rookie quarterback Bo Nix impressed on the field, and now a Colorado sports card store is ready to pay $50,000 to keep a unique card featuring him close to home.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, ABC 4

item.title: Navy veteran's service dogs denied access on plane in Denver
item.description: A Navy veteran missed his flight after not being allowed to take his service dogs on a plane from Denver to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Shaul Turner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Russian aircraft fly in Alaskan Air Defense Identification Zone, US says
item.description: Russian military aircraft were spotted flying in the Alaskan Air Defense Identification Zone (ADIZ) on Tuesday and Wednesday, U.S. officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 things to watch at this year's CPAC
item.description: Conservatives are gathering in National Harbor, Md., this week for the annual Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in what is poised to be a celebration for the political right following President Trump’s election victory in November.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KRON4

item.title: Viral video shows Indiana police punch man during arrest. Here's what we know
item.description: William Jerome Armstrong was arrested Tuesday afternoon in the parking lot of a Walmart after police say they saw his car parked in an emergency fire lane.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, Fox 5

item.title: Kennedy Center cancels Pride performance featuring Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington
item.description: The Kennedy Center canceled an upcoming performance set to feature the Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington, which the center said was due to financial constraints and scheduling conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Man who admitted to shooting Black teen at wrong door dies before sentencing
item.description: Andrew Lester, a man who pleaded guilty last week to second-degree assault after he shot Ralph Yarl, an unarmed teenage boy in Kansas City in 2023, has died, according to a Clay County prosecutor.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KRON4

item.title: Suspects charged in connection to burglaries of several pro athletes
item.description: Seven Chilean nationals face federal charges related to the burglaries of several high profile athletes' homes.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, Fox 5

item.title: Family of DC plane crash victim files $250M claims against FAA, Army
item.description: The claims were filed on behalf of the widow and children of Casey Crafton, 40, of Salem. He died as a result of the crash, leaving behind his wife of 16 years, Rachel, and three young sons.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Schaefer, Fox 5

item.title: Tens of millions of dead people aren't getting Social Security checks, despite Trump and Musk claims
item.description: The Trump administration is falsely claiming that tens of millions of dead people over 100 years old are receiving Social Security payments.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: Vehicle launches off overpass, plummets onto freeway below
item.description: One driver is lucky to be alive after their vehicle plummeted off an overpass in California on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: A new article published this week in The Lancet medical journal found that more than half of the world's population lacks access to safe and affordable medical oxygen services, and most people outside of high-income countries go without adequate oxygen services for their medical conditions.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, ABC 4

item.title: Leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to 6 killings in multiple states ordered held without bail
item.description: The Zizians have been tied to the killing of U.S. Border Patrol Agent David Maland near the Canadian border in January and five other homicides in Vermont, Pennsylvania and California.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, Fox 5
